Health.label: Life expectancy
Health.desc: Period life expectancy is the number of years the average person born in a certain year would live if they experienced the same chances of dying at each age as people did that year.
Health.source: Riley (2005); Zijdeman et al. (2015); HMD (2025); UN WPP (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Health.label: Child mortality rate
Health.desc: The long-run estimated share of newborns who die before reaching the age of five.
Health.source: Gapminder (2015); UN Inter-agency Group for Child Mortality Estimation (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Health.label: Maternal mortality rate
Health.desc: The estimated number of women who die from maternal conditions per 100,000 live births, based on data from death certificates, large-scale surveys, and statistical modeling.
Health.source: UN MMEIG (2023) and other sources – with major processing by Our World in Data

Health.label: Death rate from cardiovascular disease
Health.desc: Estimated number of age-standardized deaths from cardiovascular diseases in both sexes, per 100,000 people.
Health.source: World Health Organization (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Health.label: Share of adults who are overweight or obese
Health.source: World Health Organization - Global Health Observatory (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Health.label: Share of men who smoke
Health.desc: Estimated percentage of male adults (15 years and older) who currently use tobacco. This includes all forms of tobacco use, such as smoking, chewing or snuffing, but excludes products that do not contain tobacco, such as e-cigarettes.
Health.source: World Health Organization - Global Health Observatory (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Health.label: Tuberculosis incidence rate
Health.desc: The number of new cases of tuberculosis per 100,000 people.
Health.source: WHO (2025); Population based on various sources (2024) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Health.label: Share of the population living with HIV
Health.desc: Share of people aged 15-49 who test positive for HIV at a given time. This is the central estimate.
Health.source: Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Health.label: Alcohol consumption per person
Health.unit: liters of pure alcohol, projected estimates, 15+ years of age
Health.source: Global Health Observatory Data Repository - World Health Organization (WHO), via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Health.label: Vaccination coverage
Health.desc: Share of one-year-olds who have had three doses of the hepatitis B vaccine in a given year.
Health.source: WHO & UNICEF (2025); UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Health.label: Exposure to outdoor air pollution
Health.desc: Annual population-weighted average concentration of fine particulate matter (PM2.5) in the air, measured in micrograms per cubic meter (µg/m³). Fine particulate matter (PM2.5) are particles with a diameter of 2.5 micrometers or less, which can pose significant health risks.
Health.source: SatPM, Atmospheric Composition Analysis Group, Washington University in St. Louis (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Environment.label: CO₂ emissions per capita
Environment.desc: Carbon dioxide (CO₂) emissions from burning fossil fuels and industrial processes. This includes emissions from transport, electricity generation, and heating, but not land-use change.
Environment.source: Global Carbon Budget (2025); Population based on various sources (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Share of electricity from renewables
Environment.desc: Measured as a percentage of total electricity produced in the country or region.
Environment.source: Ember (2026); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Share of land covered by forest
Environment.desc: The share of land area covered by forest, including both natural forests and forest plantations.
Environment.source: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs (2013) and other sources – with major processing by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Outdoor air pollution
Environment.desc: Annual population-weighted average concentration of fine particulate matter (PM2.5) in the air, measured in micrograms per cubic meter (µg/m³). Fine particulate matter (PM2.5) are particles with a diameter of 2.5 micrometers or less, which can pose significant health risks.
Environment.source: SatPM, Atmospheric Composition Analysis Group, Washington University in St. Louis (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Ozone-depleting substance consumption
Environment.desc:
Consumption of various controlled substances. These include Bromochloromethane (BCM), Carbon Tetrachloride (CTC), Chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs), Hydrobromofluorocarbons (HBFCs), Hydrochlorofluorocarbons (HCFCs), Hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs), Methyl Bromide (MB), Methyl Chloroform (TCA) and Other Fully Halogenated CFCs.

We assign zero to missing values for a given (year, country, chemical) triple. This
Environment.source: UN Environment Programme (2023) – processed by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Share of energy from fossil fuels
Environment.desc: Measured as a percentage of the total primary energy, using the substitution method.
Environment.source: Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Annual temperature anomaly
Environment.desc: The difference of a specific year's average surface temperature from the 1991-2020 mean, in degrees Celsius.
Environment.source: Contains modified Copernicus Climate Change Service information (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Red List Index of biodiversity
Environment.source: BirdLife International and IUCN – processed by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Annual deaths from natural disasters
Environment.desc: Total number of dead and missing people.
Environment.source: EM-DAT, CRED / UCLouvain (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Water stress
Environment.desc: Level of water stress: freshwater withdrawal as a proportion of available freshwater resources (%)
Environment.source: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations – processed by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Access to electricity
Environment.desc: Access to electricity means having an electricity source that can provide very basic lighting, and charge a phone or power a radio for 4 hours per day.
Environment.source: Data compiled from multiple sources by the World Bank – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Access to safely managed drinking water
Environment.desc: Proportion of people using an improved drinking water source that is located on premises, available when needed and free from faecal and priority chemical contamination.
Environment.source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply, Sanitation and Hygiene (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Access to safely managed sanitation
Environment.desc: Proportion of people using improved sanitation facilities that are not shared with other households and where excreta are safely disposed in situ or transported and treated off-site.
Environment.source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply, Sanitation and Hygiene (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Access to clean cooking fuels
Environment.source: World Health Organization - Global Health Observatory (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Environment.label: Land animals slaughtered for meat
Environment.desc: This data is based on the country of production, not consumption.
Environment.source: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Economy.label: GDP per capita
Economy.desc: Average economic output per person in a country or region per year. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
Economy.source: Eurostat, OECD, IMF, and World Bank (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Economy.label: Extreme poverty rate
Economy.desc: Percentage of population living in households with an income or consumption below $3 per day.
Economy.source: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Economy.label: Median daily income
Economy.desc: Value of income or consumption per day below which 50% of the population live.
Economy.source: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Economy.label: Annual working hours per worker
Economy.desc: Average working hours per worker in a given year.
Economy.source: Feenstra et al. - Penn World Table (2025); Huberman and Minns (2005) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Economy.label: Unemployment rate
Economy.desc: Share of the labor force without work, but actively looking for a job and available to start soon.
Economy.source: ILO Modelled Estimates, via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Economy.label: Income inequality (Gini index)
Economy.desc: The Gini coefficient measures inequality on a scale from 0 to 1. Higher values indicate higher inequality.
Economy.source: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Economy.label: Female labor force participation
Economy.desc: Share of the female working-age population (ages 15 and over) who are economically active (employed or unemployed).
Economy.source: ILO Modelled Estimates, via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Economy.label: Economic output per hour worked
Economy.desc: Productivity is calculated as GDP divided by the total number of hours worked in the economy. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
Economy.source: Feenstra et al. - Penn World Table (2025) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Economy.label: Trade as a share of GDP
Economy.desc: Trade in goods and services as a share of GDP, shown as a percentage.
Economy.source: National statistical organizations and central banks, OECD national accounts, and World Bank staff estimates (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Economy.label: Share of people asked for a bribe
Economy.desc: Share of respondents who reported having contact with a public official in the past 12 months and paying or being asked to pay a bribe.
Economy.source: United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Food & Agriculture.label: Daily supply of calories per person
Food & Agriculture.desc: This data shows per capita daily calorie supply, which is the amount of calories available to an average person, and does not necessarily correspond to the calories actually consumed by that person.
Food & Agriculture.source: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (2025) and other sources – with major processing by Our World in Data

Food & Agriculture.label: Share of the labor force employed in agriculture
Food & Agriculture.source: International Labor Organization and historical sources (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Food & Agriculture.label: Cereal yields
Food & Agriculture.source: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (2025) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Food & Agriculture.label: Meat supply per person
Food & Agriculture.desc: Quantity that is available for consumption at the end of the supply chain. It does not account for consumer waste, so the quantity that is actually consumed may be lower.
Food & Agriculture.source: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Food & Agriculture.label: Share of adults who are overweight
Food & Agriculture.source: World Health Organization - Global Health Observatory (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Food & Agriculture.label: Share of land used for agriculture
Food & Agriculture.source: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Food & Agriculture.label: Vegetable consumption per person
Food & Agriculture.desc: Quantity that is available for consumption at the end of the supply chain. It does not account for consumer waste, so the quantity that is actually consumed may be lower.
Food & Agriculture.source: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Food & Agriculture.label: Share of women with anemia
Food & Agriculture.source: Global Health Observatory / World Health Statistics - World Health Organization (WHO), via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Food & Agriculture.label: Fertilizer use per hectare of cropland
Food & Agriculture.desc: Agricultural use of all fertilizer products (including nitrogenous, potash, and phosphate fertilizers) per area of cropland, which corresponds to the sum of arable land and permanent crops.
Food & Agriculture.source: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (2025) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Food & Agriculture.label: Agricultural productivity per worker
Food & Agriculture.source: World Bank (World Development Indicators) and ILO (ILOSTAT) (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Human Development Index
Living Conditions.desc: The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. Higher values indicate higher human development.
Living Conditions.source: UNDP, Human Development Report (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Access to electricity
Living Conditions.desc: Access to electricity means having an electricity source that can provide very basic lighting, and charge a phone or power a radio for 4 hours per day.
Living Conditions.source: Data compiled from multiple sources by the World Bank – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Access to safely managed drinking water
Living Conditions.desc: Proportion of people using an improved drinking water source that is located on premises, available when needed and free from faecal and priority chemical contamination.
Living Conditions.source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply, Sanitation and Hygiene (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Access to safely managed sanitation
Living Conditions.desc: Proportion of people using improved sanitation facilities that are not shared with other households and where excreta are safely disposed in situ or transported and treated off-site.
Living Conditions.source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply, Sanitation and Hygiene (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Access to clean cooking fuels
Living Conditions.source: World Health Organization - Global Health Observatory (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Annual working hours per worker
Living Conditions.desc: Average working hours per worker in a given year.
Living Conditions.source: Feenstra et al. - Penn World Table (2025); Huberman and Minns (2005) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Share of people who say they are happy
Living Conditions.desc: % of respondents replying "very happy" or "quite happy" when asked "Taking all things together, would you say you are very happy, quite happy, not very happy or not at all happy?"
Living Conditions.source: Integrated Values Surveys (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Share of people who say most people can be trusted
Living Conditions.desc: % of respondents replying "Most people can be trusted" when asked "Generally speaking, would you say that most people can be trusted or that you need to be very careful in dealing with people?" Possible answers include "Most people can be trusted", "Do not know" and "Need to be very careful".
Living Conditions.source: Integrated Values Surveys (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Productivity: output per hour worked
Living Conditions.desc: Productivity is calculated as GDP divided by the total number of hours worked in the economy. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
Living Conditions.source: Feenstra et al. - Penn World Table (2025) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Share of single-person households
Living Conditions.source: UN, national statistical agencies, OECD, and Deutschland in Daten (2021) – processed by Our World in Data

Living Conditions.label: Legalization of same-sex marriage
Living Conditions.desc: Legislation status of same-sex marriage. This is marriage and marriage recognition between two people of the same biological sex and/or gender identity.
Living Conditions.source: Equaldex (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Democracy index
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which political leaders are elected under comprehensive suffrage in free and fair elections, and freedoms of association and expression are guaranteed.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Human rights index
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which people are free from government torture, political killings, and forced labor, they have property rights, and enjoy the freedoms of movement, religion, expression, and association.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Freedom of expression
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which people can discuss political matters at home and in the public sphere, the press, and media are free and can present different political perspectives, and the freedom of academic and cultural expression.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Political corruption
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which a country is affected by political corruption.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Share of women in parliament
Rights & Democracy.desc: Percentage of women in the lower or single chamber of the legislature.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Women's political empowerment
Rights & Democracy.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which women enjoy civil liberties, can participate in civil society, and are represented in politics.
Rights & Democracy.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Legality of same-sex relations
Rights & Democracy.desc: Legal status of consensual homosexual acts among adults in private. The categories are "Legal" and "Illegal".
Rights & Democracy.source: Mignot (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Child labor rate
Rights & Democracy.desc: Share of children aged 7-14 years involved in economic activity for at least one hour in the reference week of the survey.
Rights & Democracy.source: International Labour Organization, UNICEF, and World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Rights & Democracy.label: Child marriage rate
Rights & Democracy.desc: Proportion of women aged 20-24 years who were married or in a union before age 15 (%)
Rights & Democracy.source: UNICEF – processed by Our World in Data

Violence & War.label: Homicide rate
Violence & War.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024); United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Violence & War.label: Conflict deaths
Violence & War.desc: The best estimate of the number of deaths of combatants and civilians due to fighting in interstate, intrastate, extrasystemic, non-state conflicts, and one-sided violence that were ongoing that year.
Violence & War.source: Uppsala Conflict Data Program (2026); geoBoundaries (2023) – processed by Our World in Data

Violence & War.label: Terrorism deaths
Violence & War.desc: Confirmed deaths, including all victims and attackers who died because of a terrorist attack.
Violence & War.source: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) (2022) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Violence & War.label: Military spending as a share of GDP
Violence & War.desc: Total military expenditure divided by gross domestic product, expressed as a percentage.
Violence & War.source: Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Violence & War.label: Territory controlled by government
Violence & War.desc: Central estimate of the percentage of territory controlled by the government.
Violence & War.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Violence & War.label: Armed forces personnel
Violence & War.source: The Military Balance - International Institute for Strategic Studies, via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Violence & War.label: Prevalence of female genital mutilation
Violence & War.source: UNICEF, Demographic and Health Surveys (DHS), and Multiple Indicator Cluster Surveys (MICS), via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Violence & War.label: Share of women married by age 15
Violence & War.desc: Proportion of women aged 20-24 years who were married or in a union before age 15 (%)
Violence & War.source: UNICEF – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Population
Population & Demography.desc: The total population, measured on 1 July of the year shown.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Fertility rate
Population & Demography.desc: The total fertility rate: the average number of children a woman would have if she experienced the year's age-specific birth rates throughout her childbearing years.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Life expectancy
Population & Demography.desc: Period life expectancy is the number of years the average person born in a certain year would live if they experienced the same chances of dying at each age as people did that year.
Population & Demography.source: Riley (2005); Zijdeman et al. (2015); HMD (2025); UN WPP (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Child mortality rate
Population & Demography.desc: The long-run estimated share of newborns who die before reaching the age of five.
Population & Demography.source: Gapminder (2015); UN Inter-agency Group for Child Mortality Estimation (2025) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Median age
Population & Demography.desc: The age that divides the population into two halves of equal size — half are younger and half are older.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Share of population living in urban areas
Population & Demography.desc: Share of the population living in areas classified as urban, based on each country's national definition.
Population & Demography.source: United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Population Division (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Share of the population born in another country
Population & Demography.desc: Immigrants as a share of the total population.
Population & Demography.source: United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs (2024) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Population growth rate
Population & Demography.desc: The annual rate at which the population is growing, expressed as a percentage.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Total dependency ratio
Population & Demography.desc: The number of children (under 15) and people aged 65 and over per 100 working-age people (aged 15 to 64).
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Population & Demography.label: Sex ratio at birth
Population & Demography.desc: The number of male births per 100 female births.
Population & Demography.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

Technology.label: Share of the population using the internet
Technology.desc: Share of the population who used the Internet in the last three months.
Technology.source: World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database - International Telecommunication Union (ITU), via World Bank (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Dat

Technology.label: Share of new cars sold that are electric
Technology.source: International Energy Agency. Global EV Outlook 2025. – processed by Our World in Data

Technology.label: Communication technology subscriptions per 100 people
Technology.source: International Telecommunication Union (ITU), via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Technology.label: R&D spending as a share of GDP
Technology.source: UNESCO UIS Stat Bulk Data Download Service, via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Technology.label: R&D researchers per million people
Technology.source: UNESCO UIS Stat Bulk Data Download Service, via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Technology.label: Patent applications per million people
Technology.desc: Patent applications per million people are calculated by Our World in Data based on patent data from the World Bank's World Development Indicators, and population estimates from the United Nations World Population Prospects.
Technology.source: World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO), via World Bank (2026); United Nations Population Division, national statistical offices, and Eurostat, via Worl

Technology.label: Scientific publications per million people
Technology.desc: Scientific and technical journal articles per million people. Disciplines include physics, biology, chemistry, mathematics, clinical medicine, biomedical research, engineering and technology, and earth and space sciences.
Technology.source: National Science Foundation Science and Engineering Indicators, via World Bank (2026); United Nations Population Division, national statistical offices, and Eur

Technology.label: Annual objects launched into space
Technology.desc: Annual number of satellites, probes, landers, crewed spacecrafts, and space station flight elements, launched into Earth orbit or beyond.
Technology.source: United Nations Office for Outer Space Affairs (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Technology.label: Annual clinical trials
Technology.desc: Annual number of completed clinical trials registered in the ClinicalTrials.gov database for each country. Registration in the ClinicalTrials database is mandatory for trials in the United States, but voluntary for other countries.
Technology.source: ClinicalTrials.gov (2025) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Education.label: Literacy rate
Education.desc: Share of adults who can read and write a simple statement about their everyday life.
Education.source: UNESCO (1957) and other sources – with major processing by Our World in Data

Education.label: Average years of schooling
Education.desc: Average number of years (excluding years spent repeating individual grades) adults over 25 years participated in formal education.
Education.source: UNDP, Human Development Report (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Education.label: Government spending on education as a share of GDP
Education.desc: Total annual general government spending on all levels of education, expressed as a percentage of GDP.
Education.source: UNESCO Institute for Statistics (2025); Tanzi & Schuknecht (2000) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Education.label: School enrollment rates
Education.desc: Number of children of any age group who are enrolled in primary education, expressed as a percentage of the total population of the official primary school age.
Education.source: UNESCO Institute for Statistics (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

Education.label: Gender parity in education
Education.desc: Total enrolment in tertiary education regardless of age expressed as a percentage of the population in the 5-year age group immediately following upper secondary education.
Education.source: UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS) Data API, via World Bank (2026); Lee and Lee (2016) – with major processing by Our World in Data

Education.label: Share of the population using the internet
Education.desc: Share of the population who used the Internet in the last three months.
Education.source: World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database - International Telecommunication Union (ITU), via World Bank (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Dat

Education.label: Researchers in R&D per million people
Education.source: UNESCO UIS Stat Bulk Data Download Service, via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Education.label: Research and development spending as a share of GDP
Education.source: UNESCO UIS Stat Bulk Data Download Service, via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

Education.label: Scientific publications per million people
Education.desc: Scientific and technical journal articles per million people. Disciplines include physics, biology, chemistry, mathematics, clinical medicine, biomedical research, engineering and technology, and earth and space sciences.
Education.source: National Science Foundation Science and Engineering Indicators, via World Bank (2026); United Nations Population Division, national statistical offices, and Eur

Education.label: Patent applications per million people
Education.desc: Patent applications per million people are calculated by Our World in Data based on patent data from the World Bank's World Development Indicators, and population estimates from the United Nations World Population Prospects.
Education.source: World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO), via World Bank (2026); United Nations Population Division, national statistical offices, and Eurostat, via Worl

highlights.label: Life expectancy
highlights.desc: Period life expectancy is the number of years the average person born in a certain year would live if they experienced the same chances of dying at each age as people did that year.
highlights.source: Riley (2005); Zijdeman et al. (2015); HMD (2025); UN WPP (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

highlights.label: CO₂ emissions per capita
highlights.desc: Carbon dioxide (CO₂) emissions from burning fossil fuels and industrial processes. This includes emissions from transport, electricity generation, and heating, but not land-use change.
highlights.source: Global Carbon Budget (2025); Population based on various sources (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data

highlights.label: GDP per capita
highlights.desc: Average economic output per person in a country or region per year. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
highlights.source: Eurostat, OECD, IMF, and World Bank (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

highlights.label: Daily supply of calories per person
highlights.desc: This data shows per capita daily calorie supply, which is the amount of calories available to an average person, and does not necessarily correspond to the calories actually consumed by that person.
highlights.source: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (2025) and other sources – with major processing by Our World in Data

highlights.label: Human Development Index
highlights.desc: The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. Higher values indicate higher human development.
highlights.source: UNDP, Human Development Report (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

highlights.label: Democracy index
highlights.desc: Central estimate of the extent to which political leaders are elected under comprehensive suffrage in free and fair elections, and freedoms of association and expression are guaranteed.
highlights.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

highlights.label: Homicide rate
highlights.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024); United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

highlights.label: Population
highlights.desc: The total population, measured on 1 July of the year shown.
highlights.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

highlights.label: Share of the population using the internet
highlights.desc: Share of the population who used the Internet in the last three months.
highlights.source: World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database - International Telecommunication Union (ITU), via World Bank (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Dat

highlights.label: Literacy rate
highlights.desc: Share of adults who can read and write a simple statement about their everyday life.
highlights.source: UNESCO (1957) and other sources – with major processing by Our World in Data
